skill.name: browser-use
skill.description: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction using the browser-use CLI tool.
skill.summary: Browser automation skill for Claude Code that enables navigating websites, interacting with elements, filling forms, taking screenshots, and running AI agents.
skill.author: browser-use

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 163 patterns in SKILL.md documentation file. All findings are false positives: backtick patterns are markdown code blocks documenting CLI usage, URLs are example/documentation links, and file paths reference user-controlled locations for legitimate browser profile and cookie management. The skill provides browser automation via the browser-use CLI with no malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Cookie Export/Import Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill supports exporting cookies to files and importing them, which could expose session tokens if misused. This is legitimate browser profile syncing functionality but should be documented.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Cookie export/import is documented for legitimate profile syncing between local and cloud browsers, but involves sensitive authentication data.

low_findings.title: External Command Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains extensive documentation of browser-use CLI commands. All instances are markdown code blocks for documentation purposes, not actual code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matched backticks in markdown documentation - these are example commands, not executable code in the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Multiple example URLs present in SKILL.md including github.com, example.com. These are documentation links and example values, not hardcoded endpoints for data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are clearly documentation references and example values for user guidance.

content.user_title: Automate browser interactions and web tasks
content.value_statement: Manual web testing and data extraction is time-consuming and error-prone. Browser-use provides AI-driven browser automation for reliable, repeatable web interactions.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to URLs and manage browser tabs
Click, type, and interact with web elements using indices from page state
Take screenshots (base64 or file) and inspect page state
Execute JavaScript and extract data from pages
Manage cookies with export, import, and sync functionality
Run autonomous AI agent tasks in cloud browser sessions
limitations: Requires browser-use CLI to be installed and configured on the system
Cannot bypass CAPTCHA or anti-bot detection on protected websites
Cloud browser sessions require network connectivity and may incur costs
Cookie syncing requires explicit user consent and domain specification

use_cases.title: Web QA Testing
use_cases.description: Automate end-to-end testing workflows: navigate to staging site, verify elements, fill forms, submit, and validate results with screenshots.

use_cases.title: Authenticated Data Extraction
use_cases.description: Sync browser profiles to access authenticated sites, extract data from dashboards or reports, and export results without manual login.

use_cases.title: Autonomous Research Agents
use_cases.description: Deploy cloud browser agents to research topics across multiple sites, summarize findings, and run parallel research tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Open https://example.com and show me the available clickable elements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick inspection of a webpage structure

prompt_templates.title: Form Filling Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to the login page, check the available elements, input username into field 3 and password into field 5, then click the submit button.

prompt_templates.title: Screenshot and Data Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Take a screenshot of the current page, get the page title, extract all text from element 12, and execute JavaScript to get the current URL.

prompt_templates.title: Cloud Agent Research Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Run an autonomous agent task: Search for the latest AI news on three major tech sites and summarize the top 5 stories with links.

output_examples.input: browser-use state on a search results page
output_examples.output: Page state showing URL, title, and numbered list of clickable elements like '1. Search result: Article Title', '2. Next page button'
Screenshot saved to specified file path with confirmation message and base64 preview

best_practices: Always run 'browser-use state' first to see available elements and their indices before interacting
Use domain-specific cookie sync instead of full profile sync to minimize exposure of sensitive data
Close browser sessions and stop cloud sessions after completing tasks to free resources
anti_patterns: Do not use full cookie profile sync without explicit user consent - always specify domains
Do not reuse sessions after stopping tasks - create new sessions instead
Do not run cloud agent tasks without monitoring - check status periodically and stop stuck tasks

faq.question: What is browser-use and how do I install it?
faq.answer: Browser-use is a CLI tool for browser automation. Run 'browser-use doctor' to check installation status and see setup instructions.

faq.question: How do I use this skill with my existing browser login sessions?
faq.answer: Use '--browser real --profile ProfileName' for local Chrome profiles, or sync cookies to a cloud profile using 'browser-use profile sync --from ProfileName --domain example.com'.

faq.question: What is the difference between chromium, real, and remote browser modes?
faq.answer: Chromium is fast and headless by default. Real uses your local Chrome with existing logins. Remote runs in the cloud with proxy support and AI agent capabilities.

faq.question: How do I run autonomous AI agent tasks?
faq.answer: Use 'browser-use -b remote run task description' to launch an AI agent task. Poll status with 'browser-use task status task-id' and stop with 'browser-use task stop task-id'.

faq.question: Can I expose my local dev server to a cloud browser?
faq.answer: Yes, use 'browser-use tunnel port' to create a Cloudflare tunnel URL, then open that URL with '--browser remote'.

faq.question: How do I clean up browser sessions and resources?
faq.answer: Run 'browser-use close' for local sessions, 'browser-use session stop --all' for cloud sessions, and 'browser-use tunnel stop --all' for tunnels.
